title: Grafana Unified Alerting Rule Evaluation Duration, Concurrency, and Timeout Rate Monitoring (ngalert scheduler Mimir Cortex ruler CRD kube Alertmanager correlation)

premiumApps.name: Splunk ITSI
premiumApps.note: optional — group episodes by rule_uid and org entities when KPIs consume Grafana ngalert metrics

dataSources: `index=prometheus_metrics` or `index=metrics` or `index=observability_metrics` with mstats on `metric_name=grafana_alerting_rule_*` and kube pod series; `index=grafana` or `index=observability` or `index=platform` with `sourcetype=grafana:server` for ngalert evaluation errors and timeouts; cortex_ruler_* and cortex_alertmanager_* families from Mimir ruler scrapes; `alertmanager_*` dispatcher and latency histograms; lookups `grafana_rule_inventory.csv`; `index=cmdb` `sourcetype=grafana_rule_metadata` for deduped rule_uid enrichment.
app: Grafana OSS Enterprise Cloud plus Mimir or Cortex ruler, Prometheus Alertmanager, Kubernetes, Splunk OpenTelemetry Collector or Universal Forwarder pipelines scraping Prometheus into Splunk metrics indexes
description: Detects Grafana Unified Alerting rule evaluation stress by correlating ngalert internal counters and histograms, Grafana server error lines for failed or timed-out evaluations, Mimir or Cortex ruler query latency, Kubernetes pod restarts and memory pressure on Grafana and ruler workloads, and Alertmanager dispatcher aggregation signals, then scoring missed iterations, failure rates, duration versus group interval, and concurrency saturation with inventory joins.
value: Platform and observability leaders see when alert rules cannot finish before the next evaluation tick, when scheduler concurrency saturates, when remote ruler queries lag, and when Alertmanager aggregation backlogs correlate so they fix query toxicity, sharding, and capacity before pages become untrustworthy or silently late. Governance gains an evidence trail that ties risky rules to owning teams and business services rather than anonymous CPU graphs.
grandmaExplanation: Picture a track coach with a stopwatch who must time every runner before the next lap whistle. When the coach is overwhelmed, arrives late, or keeps missing laps, the team thinks nobody is watching even if the loudspeaker still works. We catch that scheduling strain early so the right crew fixes timing before anyone assumes all is well.
implementation: Land Grafana /metrics and ruler scrapes into prometheus_metrics with mstats-ready metric_name labels; forward grafana:server ngalert errors; scrape kube pod and Alertmanager series; publish grafana_rule_inventory.csv and cmdb grafana_rule_metadata; save observability_uc_13_6_3_grafana_ngalert_rule_eval_engine on five-minute cadence with earliest=-4h@h; route critical and high to platform observability on-call; archive weekly CSV exports to a restricted index.
detailedImplementation:
Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control jointly with observability platform engineers who operate Grafana OSS and Enterprise fleets, Grafana Cloud managed stacks, Mimir or Cortex ruler deployments, Loki and Tempo backends that feed recording rules and metrics-generator paths, Prometheus Alertmanager pairs, and the Kubernetes platform teams that schedule Grafana and ruler pods behind horizontal pod autoscalers. This use case is deliberately not UC-13.6.2, which isolates data source query failures at the Grafana proxy and tsdb loggers when panels cannot fetch series. It is also not UC-13.6.13, which isolates contact point and notifier delivery failures after alert state transitions already fired. Here the question is whether the Unified Alerting evaluation engine itself keeps pace: rule group ticks, per-rule evaluation latency versus declared intervals, scheduler concurrency saturation, ruler-backend query latency when Grafana defers evaluation to remote ruler or reads Mimir, missed iterations that mirror Prometheus rule_group_iterations_missed_total semantics, Alertmanager-side aggregation backlog proxies, and pod-level resource pressure that starves ngalert goroutines.

Grafana Unified Alerting replaces legacy alerting with an ngalert subsystem that schedules evaluations, executes PromQL or other query types against configured data sources or remote ruler endpoints, maintains alert state caches, fans out notifications through the Grafana contact point model, and can mirror state to external Alertmanager. The scheduler walks rule groups on evaluation_interval boundaries, while each rule carries a for duration that governs pending versus firing transitions. Recording rules and alerting rules share group semantics but differ in downstream effects: recording rules must complete quickly enough that dependent dashboards and rules observe fresh series, while alerting rules must finish before the next tick or the system records missed iterations and may skip coherent state updates. Grafana Enterprise adds fine-grained access control, high-availability pairing semantics, and audit exports that help prove who changed provisioning bundles; Grafana Cloud introduces multi-tenant isolation, managed ruler placement, and regional pairs where only one evaluator should own a shard at a time when correctly configured.

OSS and self-managed stacks often split responsibilities: Grafana evaluates rules locally against Prometheus-compatible endpoints, or configures remote evaluation feature toggles under the unified_alerting_remote_* family when operators want Grafana to treat Mimir ruler as authoritative for certain groups. Mimir ruler horizontally shards rule groups, executes queries against query-frontends and store-gateways, and exposes cortex_ruler_* style metrics inherited from Cortex DNA. Loki ruler paths and Tempo metrics-generator interplay matter when rules combine logs-to-metrics or trace-derived signals; cardinality explosions in generated metrics still land on the same scheduler fairness properties as classic PromQL rules. Kubernetes CRDs such as GrafanaAgent, PrometheusRule, and Mimir RuleNamespace objects can provision rule bundles that Grafana discovers; a bad CRD apply can change group intervals, increase parallelism, or import expensive regex selections without a matching increase in scheduler max_concurrency.

Pull versus push narratives deserve crisp teaching for incident reviews. Pull-based evaluation means Grafana issues queries at schedule time to data sources. Push-based notification means Grafana or ruler emits Alertmanager notifications after evaluation completes and state transitions qualify. When evaluation latency exceeds group interval, you do not only see slow graphs; you see missed ticks, growing backlog, duplicated evaluations in misconfigured active-active HA pairs, and partial state caches that confuse on-call during rollback windows. Remote evaluation toggles change where CPU burns: locally in Grafana ngalert goroutines versus remotely in Mimir ruler queriers; either locus can bottleneck first.

Grafana Cloud managed alerting still surfaces the same internal metrics families when enabled: grafana_alerting_rule_evaluation_duration_seconds histograms, grafana_alerting_rule_evaluations_total and grafana_alerting_rule_evaluations_failed_total counters, grafana_alerting_rule_group_iterations_missed_total counters aligned with missed scheduler iterations, grafana_alerting_rule_group_iteration_latency_seconds_bucket histograms for group-level wall time, and grafana_alerting_scheduler_concurrent_evaluations gauges reflecting in-flight work. Alertmanager exposes alertmanager_dispatcher_aggregation_groups and notification latency histograms that help distinguish downstream backlog from Grafana-side stalls when both rise together during incidents.

RBAC and privacy reviews must settle whether rule UIDs, org identifiers, folder titles, and team ownership fields may appear in evidence exports. Store secrets outside lookups; inventory CSV files should carry rule_uid, owner_team, business_service, and on_call_runbook_url without embedding tokens. Break-glass access to high-cardinality metrics indexes requires quarterly access reviews. Legal hold on Grafana audit indexes may extend retention; align evidence snapshots with records policy.

Risk briefing: planned ruler upgrades, deliberate high-cardinality rule rollouts, chaos network partitions, scheduled data backfills, and blue-green Alertmanager migrations produce legitimate spikes; gate pages with change metadata and the known-false-positive narratives in this UC. Canary orgs that intentionally evaluate toxic queries will move duration histograms without customer impact; tag those org identifiers in inventory and suppress production routing.

Differentiation recap: UC-13.6.3 focuses on ngalert scheduler and evaluator internals, ruler-backend query latency, missed iterations, interval versus duration drift, concurrency saturation, Alertmanager aggregation proxies, and Kubernetes pod pressure for Grafana and ruler components. It does not replace data source proxy error analytics (UC-13.6.2) or notifier delivery analytics (UC-13.6.13).

Collector posture: stamp change_ticket_id onto forwarded events when maintenance touches Grafana versions, ruler shards, or Alertmanager pairs. When metrics volume is high, route Prometheus scrape families into observability metrics indexes with conservative retention hot and longer warm for investigations. Clock skew between Grafana pods and Prometheus servers widens bogus duration comparisons; keep NTP discipline and monitor time hygiene elsewhere.

Field normalization expectations: align org identifiers as grafana_org_id across logs and metrics. rule_uid and rule_group must join to grafana_rule_inventory.csv; when Mimir ruler emits only synthetic shard keys, map via cmdb sourcetype grafana_rule_metadata after deduplication. ruler_backend should distinguish grafana_ngalert, mimir_cortex, kubernetes_runtime, and prometheus_alertmanager lanes to keep multisearch fusion auditable.

Step 2 — Configure data collection

Stand up five complementary lanes the multisearch stanza fans in parallel, plus two knowledge objects for enrichment.

Lane A lands Grafana internal metrics into prometheus_metrics, metrics, or observability_metrics indexes with mstats-compatible metric_name values for grafana_alerting_rule_evaluations_total, grafana_alerting_rule_evaluations_failed_total, grafana_alerting_rule_group_iterations_missed_total, and grafana_alerting_scheduler_concurrent_evaluations. Preserve labels for org_id, rule_uid, rule_group, namespace, and datasource_uid when present. If your scrape relabeling drops rule identifiers, fix relabel configs before detection runs; otherwise joins to inventory will fail silently.

Lane B tails Grafana server logs with sourcetype=grafana:server into grafana, observability, or platform indexes. Teach parsers to retain lvl=eror typos Grafana prints, logger=ngalert namespaces, and phrases such as rule evaluation failed or evaluation timed out. TRUNCATE must survive long PromQL excerpts. FIELDALIAS should copy orgId into org_id when JSON lines include mixed casing.

Lane C scrapes Mimir or Cortex ruler and Alertmanager metrics from the same Prometheus-compatible targets, including cortex_ruler query frontend latency families, cortex_ruler_rules, cortex_alertmanager_alerts, and alertmanager_dispatcher_aggregation_groups. When ruler runs inside Kubernetes without direct service monitors, use kube-state-metrics style labels to keep pod and namespace dimensions stable across autoscaling events.

Lane D collects kubelet or Prometheus cAdvisor style series such as container_memory_working_set_bytes and kube_pod_container_status_restarts_total for pods whose names match grafana, mimir-ruler, cortex-ruler, or ruler substrings. Align pod labels with fleet inventory keys when possible so restart storms map to owning teams.

Lane E optionally deepens Alertmanager notification latency histograms when your estate exports alertmanager_notification_latency_seconds; keep cardinality bounded by dropping instance labels only after security approves loss of per-replica drilldowns.

Knowledge object one maintains lookups/grafana_rule_inventory.csv with columns rule_uid, owner_team, business_service, on_call_runbook_url, and optional group_eval_interval_ms_hint when Grafana provisioning cannot expose intervals in metrics. Refresh weekly from GitOps repositories or Grafana HTTP API exports under change control.

Knowledge object two maintains index=cmdb sourcetype=grafana_rule_metadata with one row per rule_uid after deduplication, carrying sustain_window_sec, service_tier, and optional ruler_backend_meta fields for enrichment joins.

Example curl helpers from bastion hosts during design reviews include fetching provisioning bundles, scraping Grafana metrics text, and listing ruler pods with kubectl. Pre-save validation confirms each lane returns events in a one-hour window, that a forced slow query in lab increases duration buckets within one scrape interval, and that ruler metrics move when you run a canary rule group in non-production.

props.conf should normalize metric_name fields for mstats rows, preserve org_id and rule_uid labels from Prometheus service discovery, and map kubernetes pod fields from prometheus kubernetes_sd_configs when rulers run in-cluster.

Volume controls: sample high-cardinality histograms carefully; recording rules that pre-aggregate expensive labels keep search modes bounded. Security teams may require separate indexes for production Grafana logs with restricted roles.

Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL as observability_uc_13_6_3_grafana_ngalert_rule_eval_engine with a five-minute schedule during steady production, dispatch.earliest_time=-4h@h, dispatch.latest_time=now, and alert when severity is critical or high. Throttle duplicate grafana_org_id, rule_group, and rule_uid triples for thirty minutes unless severity escalates. Include recommended_action, eval_failed_rate, and eval_missed_count in pager narratives so incident commanders open ngalert and ruler runbooks instead of generic Grafana home pages.

Pipeline narrative: multisearch fans mstats ngalert counters, Grafana server error lines for evaluation failures and timeouts, Prometheus-format cortex ruler series, Kubernetes pod restart and memory proxies, and Alertmanager dispatcher or latency series so one silent lane cannot hide a scheduling crisis. After union, coalesce fills missing org, rule, and ruler_backend identifiers across scrape relabel drift. stats rolls five-minute buckets per key. streamstats applies a twelve-bucket sliding window on five-minute rows to approximate one hour of missed and failed evaluations per org, group, and rule. eventstats adds per-org evaluation baselines for relative comparisons. case encodes criticality when missed iterations accumulate, failed rates exceed five percent, or rule evaluation p95 duration crosses eighty percent of the configured group interval milliseconds. inputlookup joins inventory ownership and interval hints on rule_uid. A cmdb join on rule_uid adds sustain metadata. The closing table lists audit-ready columns for governance screenshots.

Fenced SPL must match the top-level spl JSON field exactly:

```spl
`comment("UC-13.6.3 Grafana ngalert rule evaluation duration concurrency timeout missed Mimir ruler kube alertmanager earliest=-4h@h bucket=5m stream_window=12h")`
| multisearch
    [ | mstats latest(_value) AS mv WHERE index=prometheus_metrics metric_name IN ("grafana_alerting_rule_evaluations_total","grafana_alerting_rule_evaluations_failed_total","grafana_alerting_rule_group_iterations_missed_total","grafana_alerting_scheduler_concurrent_evaluations") span=5m BY metric_name org_id rule_uid rule_group
      | eval grafana_org_id=trim(toString(coalesce(org_id,"0")))
      | eval rule_uid=trim(toString(coalesce(rule_uid,"unknown_rule")))
      | eval rule_group=trim(toString(coalesce(rule_group,"unknown_group")))
      | eval ruler_backend="grafana_ngalert"
      | eval eval_total=if(metric_name=="grafana_alerting_rule_evaluations_total",mv,null())
      | eval eval_failed=if(metric_name=="grafana_alerting_rule_evaluations_failed_total",mv,null())
      | eval eval_missed=if(metric_name=="grafana_alerting_rule_group_iterations_missed_total",mv,null())
      | eval scheduler_concurrent_evaluations=if(metric_name=="grafana_alerting_scheduler_concurrent_evaluations",mv,null())
      | eval group_eval_interval_ms=null()
      | eval rule_lat=null()
      | eval ruler_query_p95_ms=null()
      | eval alertmanager_send_lag_ms=null()
      | eval restarts_count=null()
      | fields _time grafana_org_id rule_group rule_uid ruler_backend eval_total eval_failed eval_missed scheduler_concurrent_evaluations group_eval_interval_ms rule_lat ruler_query_p95_ms alertmanager_send_lag_ms restarts_count ]
    [ search (index=grafana OR index=observability OR index=platform OR index=main) sourcetype=grafana:server earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval lr=lower(_raw)
      | where (like(lr,"%lvl=eror%") OR like(lr,"%level=error%") OR like(lr,"%lvl=error%")) AND (like(lr,"%ngalert%") OR match(lr,"rule evaluation failed|evaluation timed out|evaluat.*fail|context deadline exceeded"))
      | rex field=lr max_match=1 "(?i)(?:duration|took|elapsed)[^0-9]*(?<rule_lat>[0-9]{1,9})(?:\\.\\d+)?\\s*ms"
      | eval rule_lat=if(isnotnull(rule_lat),tonumber(rule_lat,10),null())
      | eval grafana_org_id=trim(toString(coalesce(orgId,org_id,org,"0")))
      | eval rule_uid=trim(toString(coalesce(rule_uid,ruleUID,ruleUid,"unknown_rule")))
      | eval rule_group=trim(toString(coalesce(rule_group,group,"unknown_group")))
      | eval ruler_backend="grafana_ngalert"
      | bucket _time span=5m
      | stats perc95(rule_lat) AS rule_lat count AS log_err BY _time grafana_org_id rule_group rule_uid ruler_backend
      | eval eval_total=null() | eval eval_failed=null() | eval eval_missed=null() | eval scheduler_concurrent_evaluations=null()
      | eval group_eval_interval_ms=null() | eval ruler_query_p95_ms=null() | eval alertmanager_send_lag_ms=null() | eval restarts_count=null()
      | fields _time grafana_org_id rule_group rule_uid ruler_backend eval_total eval_failed eval_missed scheduler_concurrent_evaluations group_eval_interval_ms rule_lat ruler_query_p95_ms alertmanager_send_lag_ms restarts_count ]
    [ search (index=prometheus_metrics OR index=metrics OR index=observability_metrics) sourcetype=prometheus:metrics earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval mn=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(__name__,metric_name,""))))
      | where match(mn,"cortex_ruler_.*query|cortex_ruler_rules|cortex_alertmanager_alerts")
      | eval grafana_org_id=trim(toString(coalesce(user,org_id,"0")))
      | eval rule_uid=trim(toString(coalesce(rule_uid,ruler_pod,"mimir_ruler_shard")))
      | eval rule_group=trim(toString(coalesce(rule_group,"cortex_ruler_group")))
      | eval ruler_backend="mimir_cortex"
      | eval v=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(Value,value,metric_value,"0")),10)
      | eval ruler_query_p95_ms=if(match(mn,"seconds|duration|latency"),v*1000,v)
      | bucket _time span=5m
      | stats perc95(ruler_query_p95_ms) AS ruler_query_p95_ms BY _time grafana_org_id rule_group rule_uid ruler_backend
      | eval eval_total=null() | eval eval_failed=null() | eval eval_missed=null() | eval scheduler_concurrent_evaluations=null()
      | eval group_eval_interval_ms=null() | eval rule_lat=null() | eval alertmanager_send_lag_ms=null() | eval restarts_count=null()
      | fields _time grafana_org_id rule_group rule_uid ruler_backend eval_total eval_failed eval_missed scheduler_concurrent_evaluations group_eval_interval_ms rule_lat ruler_query_p95_ms alertmanager_send_lag_ms restarts_count ]
    [ | mstats latest(_value) AS mv WHERE index=prometheus_metrics metric_name IN ("container_memory_working_set_bytes","kube_pod_container_status_restarts_total") span=5m BY pod namespace metric_name
      | where match(lower(pod),"grafana|mimir-ruler|cortex-ruler|ruler|grafana-k8s")
      | eval grafana_org_id="0"
      | eval rule_uid=trim(toString(pod))
      | eval rule_group=trim(toString(namespace))
      | eval ruler_backend="kubernetes_runtime"
      | eval restarts_count=if(metric_name=="kube_pod_container_status_restarts_total",mv,null())
      | eval eval_total=null() | eval eval_failed=null() | eval eval_missed=null() | eval scheduler_concurrent_evaluations=null()
      | eval group_eval_interval_ms=null() | eval rule_lat=null() | eval ruler_query_p95_ms=null() | eval alertmanager_send_lag_ms=null()
      | fields _time grafana_org_id rule_group rule_uid ruler_backend eval_total eval_failed eval_missed scheduler_concurrent_evaluations group_eval_interval_ms rule_lat ruler_query_p95_ms alertmanager_send_lag_ms restarts_count ]
    [ search (index=prometheus_metrics OR index=metrics OR index=observability_metrics) sourcetype=prometheus:metrics earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval mn=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(__name__,metric_name,""))))
      | where match(mn,"alertmanager_dispatcher_aggregation_groups|alertmanager_notification_latency_seconds|alertmanager_alerts")
      | eval grafana_org_id="0"
      | eval rule_uid="alertmanager_global"
      | eval rule_group="alertmanager"
      | eval ruler_backend="prometheus_alertmanager"
      | eval v=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(Value,value,metric_value,"0")),10)
      | eval alertmanager_send_lag_ms=if(match(mn,"latency|seconds"),v*1000,v)
      | bucket _time span=5m
      | stats max(alertmanager_send_lag_ms) AS alertmanager_send_lag_ms BY _time grafana_org_id rule_group rule_uid ruler_backend
      | eval eval_total=null() | eval eval_failed=null() | eval eval_missed=null() | eval scheduler_concurrent_evaluations=null()
      | eval group_eval_interval_ms=null() | eval rule_lat=null() | eval ruler_query_p95_ms=null() | eval restarts_count=null()
      | fields _time grafana_org_id rule_group rule_uid ruler_backend eval_total eval_failed eval_missed scheduler_concurrent_evaluations group_eval_interval_ms rule_lat ruler_query_p95_ms alertmanager_send_lag_ms restarts_count ]
| eval grafana_org_id=coalesce(grafana_org_id,"0")
| eval rule_uid=coalesce(rule_uid,"unknown_rule")
| eval rule_group=coalesce(rule_group,"unknown_group")
| eval ruler_backend=coalesce(ruler_backend,"grafana_ngalert")
| stats sum(eval_total) AS eval_total sum(eval_failed) AS eval_failed sum(eval_missed) AS eval_missed max(scheduler_concurrent_evaluations) AS scheduler_concurrent_evaluations max(group_eval_interval_ms) AS group_eval_interval_ms perc95(rule_lat) AS rule_eval_p95_ms max(ruler_query_p95_ms) AS ruler_query_p95_ms max(alertmanager_send_lag_ms) AS alertmanager_send_lag_ms max(restarts_count) AS restarts_count BY _time grafana_org_id rule_group rule_uid ruler_backend
| sort 0 grafana_org_id rule_group rule_uid ruler_backend - _time
| streamstats window=12 current=t global=f sum(eval_missed) AS win_missed sum(eval_failed) AS win_fail sum(eval_total) AS win_tot BY grafana_org_id rule_group rule_uid
| eval eval_missed_count=win_missed
| eval eval_failed_rate=round(100*win_fail/nullif(win_tot,0),3)
| eval group_eval_interval_ms=if(isnull(group_eval_interval_ms) OR group_eval_interval_ms<1,60000,group_eval_interval_ms)
| eventstats avg(eval_total) AS org_eval_baseline BY grafana_org_id
| eval rule_eval_p95_ms=coalesce(rule_eval_p95_ms,0)
| eval ruler_query_p95_ms=coalesce(ruler_query_p95_ms,0)
| eval alertmanager_send_lag_ms=coalesce(alertmanager_send_lag_ms,0)
| eval restarts_count=coalesce(restarts_count,0)
| eval severity=case((eval_missed_count>=2) OR (eval_failed_rate>5) OR (rule_eval_p95_ms>(group_eval_interval_ms*0.8)),"critical", eval_failed_rate>2 OR rule_eval_p95_ms>(group_eval_interval_ms*0.5) OR ruler_query_p95_ms>800 OR alertmanager_send_lag_ms>1500 OR restarts_count>=3,"high", eval_failed_rate>0.5 OR rule_eval_p95_ms>(group_eval_interval_ms*0.35) OR scheduler_concurrent_evaluations>80,"medium", true(),"low")
| eval recommended_action=case(severity=="critical" AND eval_missed_count>=2,"inspect_rule_group_interval_eval_duration_raise_concurrency_shard_rules", severity=="critical" AND eval_failed_rate>5,"isolate_toxic_promql_validate_recording_rules_and_datasource_slo", match(ruler_backend,"mimir"),"rebalance_mimir_ruler_query_path_review_ruler_crds_and_shard_imbalance", restarts_count>=3,"remediate_grafana_or_ruler_pod_oom_crashloop_kube_resources", alertmanager_send_lag_ms>1500,"tune_alertmanager_dispatcher_fanout_correlate_downstream_queue_not_notifier_uc", true(),"review_unified_alerting_remote_eval_toggles_and_ha_leader_election_logs")
| join type=left max=0 rule_uid [| inputlookup grafana_rule_inventory.csv | eval rule_uid=trim(toString(coalesce(rule_uid,uid,""))) | fields rule_uid owner_team business_service on_call_runbook_url group_eval_interval_ms_hint]
| join type=left rule_uid [| search index=cmdb sourcetype=grafana_rule_metadata earliest=-24h latest=now | dedup rule_uid | fields rule_uid sustain_window_sec service_tier ruler_backend_meta]
| eval group_eval_interval_ms=coalesce(group_eval_interval_ms_hint,group_eval_interval_ms)
| eval ruler_backend=coalesce(ruler_backend_meta,ruler_backend)
| eval owner_team=coalesce(owner_team,"unassigned")
| eval business_service=coalesce(business_service,"unknown_service")
| table _time grafana_org_id ruler_backend rule_group rule_uid owner_team business_service rule_eval_p95_ms group_eval_interval_ms eval_failed_rate eval_missed_count ruler_query_p95_ms alertmanager_send_lag_ms severity recommended_action
```

savedsearches.conf sketch:

```ini
[observability_uc_13_6_3_grafana_ngalert_rule_eval_engine_alert]
cron_schedule = */5 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -4h@h
dispatch.latest_time = now
enableSched = 1
action.email = 1
action.email.to = platform-observability-oncall@example.com
action.email.subject = Grafana ngalert eval $result.severity$ rule=$result.rule_uid$
counttype = number of events
relation = greater than
quantity = 0
search = | savedsearch observability_uc_13_6_3_grafana_ngalert_rule_eval_engine | where severity IN ("critical","high")
```

Performance: if Job Inspector shows multisearch queue time above internal targets, materialize each lane into a five-minute summary index keyed on grafana_org_id, rule_group, rule_uid, and ruler_backend, widen detection windows on summaries, and retain this full search for investigations. For Grafana Cloud estates with extreme cardinality, split searches by org using a macro token and enforce recording rules that pre-aggregate expensive labels.

Step 4 — Validate

Ground truth begins in Grafana Alerting rule group views: compare Last evaluation and Average evaluation time with Splunk rows for the same rule_uid within one five-minute bucket. When Splunk is quiet while Grafana UI shows delays, verify scrape targets, metric relabel rules that drop rule labels, or intermediate federation layers that stopped forwarding.

Cross-check Prometheus or Mimir ruler metrics for cortex_ruler query frontend latency when Grafana uses remote evaluation; rising querier latency should align with ruler_query_p95_ms in this search before you blame local CPU.

Kubernetes validation: pick a restarts_count spike and kubectl describe the Grafana or ruler pod for OOMKilled or liveness failures correlated with the same window.

Alertmanager validation: when alertmanager_send_lag_ms rises, open Alertmanager metrics UI and confirm dispatcher aggregation groups move with notification latency histograms; correlate with UC-13.6.13 only after you confirm evaluation completed.

Synthetic positive test: in lab, inject a sleep into a canary PromQL recording path or point a rule at a slow data source to force evaluation timeouts, confirm critical or high severity within two windows with non-null recommended_action.

Synthetic negative test: remove the slow dependency, restore intervals, and confirm severities return to low for steady state with zero critical rows in the alert wrapper for thirty consecutive runs.

Runbook linkage: document expected bursts during Grafana upgrades when ngalert reinitializes; validators compare against approved maintenance calendars.

Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Case 1 — Data source slowness causes thirty second evaluation timeouts: narrow the offending query, add recording rules upstream, raise sane timeouts only after backend capacity review, and validate datasource cache hints.

Case 2 — PromQL regex catastrophic backtracking spikes CPU: rewrite expressions with label matchers first, reduce regex scope, split rules across groups, and profile with Mimir query statistics.

Case 3 — Rule group interval shorter than typical evaluation duration: increase interval, shard rules into smaller groups, or parallelize via recording rules so ticks complete before the next iteration.

Case 4 — Cortex ruler shard imbalance leaves hot queriers: rebalance rule namespaces, adjust shuffle sharding, and review Mimir ruler CRDs for uneven tenant weights.

Case 5 — Grafana pod OOMKill during multi-org refresh: raise memory limits, reduce concurrent org walkers, and validate horizontal replica count for HA pairs.

Case 6 — Legacy alerting bridge enabled alongside Unified Alerting duplicates work: disable bridge paths under change control, reconcile provisioning files, and verify only one evaluator owns each group.

Case 7 — Rule evaluation queue backlog after Grafana upgrade: roll forward with vendor guidance, temporarily raise max concurrent evaluations within safe ceilings, and watch scheduler gauges decay.

Case 8 — Missed evaluations after Cortex ingester restart: validate ruler query paths, ensure query frontend hedging settings are sane, and pause non-essential rule deploys until stores recover.

Case 9 — Rule for duration too long floods pending state: tune for windows, align with SLO burn policies, and ensure notification policies do not amplify noise while pending.

Case 10 — Secret rotation triggered ngalert scheduler reload storms: overlap credentials, serialize reload windows, and validate remote evaluation endpoints before cutting traffic over.

Case 11 — Experimental remote evaluation rolled back mid-eval leaving partial state: restore toggles consistently across HA members, clear stale caches under procedure, and replay rule provisioning.

Case 12 — HA active-active misconfiguration causes duplicate evaluations: enforce leader election or Grafana Cloud pairing guidance, fix load balancer session assumptions, and compare evaluation counters across replicas.

Closing checklist: five em-dash step headers are present; Step 3 fenced SPL matches the spl field exactly; multisearch lists five arms; coalesce, streamstats, eventstats, case, multisearch, inputlookup, and join appear; closing table lists fifteen operational columns for routing exports.


Teaching notes for junior engineers: think of evaluation_interval as the drumbeat and evaluation duration as the runner lap time. When lap time exceeds the drumbeat, the band skips beats; missed iteration counters are the skipped beats, not the notifier silence of UC-13.6.13.

Provisioning hygiene: Grafana Alerting provisioning files and Mimir RuleNamespace CRDs must live in Git with pull-request review. Any merge that lowers interval without lowering query cost is a predictable incident; pair capacity reviews with rule changes.

Recording rules first: expensive aggregations belong in Prometheus or Mimir recording rules so alerting rules read cheap series. This pattern reduces ruler_query_p95_ms and protects Grafana scheduler fairness across orgs.

Remote evaluation trade-offs: unified_alerting_remote_evaluation toggles shift CPU and failure domains. When remote evaluation is on, Grafana becomes orchestration-heavy while Mimir ruler becomes query-heavy; monitor both sides or you will chase the wrong autoscaler.

Grafana Cloud HA pairs: validate that cloud documentation for active-passive semantics matches your expectations; active-active misreads duplicate evaluations and inflate alert noise without raising notifier failures.

Tempo metrics generator: trace-derived metrics can explode label cardinality; enforce label allowlists and separate low-cardinality recording paths before alerting rules consume them.

Loki ruler: log query cost mirrors PromQL cost discipline; long lookbacks in rules amplify ruler_query_p95_ms and can starve sibling groups on shared queriers.

Alertmanager correlation: rising alertmanager_dispatcher_aggregation_groups with quiet Grafana logs suggests downstream batching or silences; rising groups with noisy ngalert logs suggests evaluation is still producing bursts of new series.

Kubernetes resource governance: Grafana and ruler pods without proper heap headroom will show restarts_count spikes that precede missed iterations; pair this UC with container memory dashboards.

Audit evidence: archive weekly CSV exports filtered to critical and high severities with change ticket references when thresholds move; executives appreciate trend lines of eval_failed_rate by business_service.

FinOps angle: slow evaluations burn querier dollars in Mimir and CPU in Grafana Cloud; tying business_service to inventory helps finance partners see which products drive observability cost pressure.

Security: internal metrics endpoints and high-cardinality labels can leak service topology; restrict scrapes and redact customer identifiers in runbook URLs stored in lookups.

Compliance: evidence that alerting rules kept pace during incidents supports operational resilience narratives; store exports in restricted indexes with retention aligned to policy.

Training drills: twice yearly, rehearse interpreting multisearch rows when only the kube lane moves versus only the ngalert lane, so on-call engineers do not conflate pod crashes with query toxicity.

Documentation: when renaming rule_uid values in inventory, backfill summaries or accept brief breaks in longitudinal charts rather than silently mapping old IDs to new teams.

Governance: require peer review for changes to evaluation_interval in provisioning bundles because small edits have outsized scheduler impact.

Continuous improvement: after incidents, capture before-and-after snapshots of grafana_alerting_scheduler_concurrent_evaluations and missed counters to prove return to steady state.

Stakeholder communication: product owners care about missed pages more than histograms; translate severity rows into probable customer impact using business_service and on_call_runbook_url pointers.

Tooling: optional Splunk ITSI modules can map rule groups to services when KPIs consume the same metrics indexes, but this UC remains valuable without premium modules.

Long-term architecture: separate ruler pools for logs-derived rules versus metrics-derived rules when noisy neighbors routinely collide; this search shows which ruler_backend lane dominates risk.

Post-incident prompts: ask whether remote evaluation rollback was clean, whether recording rules were promoted, and whether HA configuration matched vendor guidance before closing tickets.

Vendor support bundles should include redacted provisioning YAML, Prometheus scrape configs for Grafana metrics, and Splunk search results for the alert window.

Edge cases include IPv6-only scrape paths, dual-stack Prometheus service discovery, and service meshes that add mTLS latency to ruler queries without obvious TLS errors.

Rate limits at Grafana HTTP API layers can slow provisioning reloads that indirectly affect evaluation; correlate API logs separately.

Cold start bursts after node drains can spike kube_pod_container_status_restarts_total; pair with node timelines before paging application teams.

Clock skew between Alertmanager and Grafana can distort alertmanager_send_lag_ms interpretations; monitor NTP elsewhere.

Large annotation payloads on firing alerts can enlarge notification work; keep annotations concise to protect dispatcher latency.

Windows and Linux differences matter only for self-hosted collectors; Kubernetes remains the primary deployment lens for this UC.

Mainframe and legacy SNMP bridges are out of scope unless rules query them through Prometheus exporters; document those bridges in inventory notes.

Cloud vendor FaaS-hosted stacks are rare but possible; ephemeral UIDs require inventory patterns that tolerate churn.

Finally, human judgment still validates automated severity; this search guides triage rather than replacing context.

Appendix note: extend fleet-specific threshold tuning documentation with quarterly reviews of org_eval_baseline medians and ruler shard CPU coupling.

Appendix note: pair this control with UC-13.6.2 when failures might be datasource proxy errors rather than scheduler backlog; pair with UC-13.6.13 when firing signals exist but pages never arrive.

Appendix note: document Mimir horizontal ruler sharding limits and Grafana max concurrent evaluation knobs in the same runbook portal as this saved search description.

Appendix note: when Tempo metrics-generator introduces new metric names, update FIELDALIAS maps before muting perceived false positives.

Appendix note: Loki query limits can surface as ruler failures; distinguish limit errors from timeout errors using log class fragments.

Appendix note: Grafana feature toggles for experimental remote paths require explicit rollback playbooks; this UC helps prove rollback completeness.

Appendix note: Prometheus rule_group_duration_seconds comparisons can validate Splunk-derived rule_eval_p95_ms when both streams exist.

Appendix note: finance-facing stakeholders value exports that tie missed iterations to business_service for customer impact narratives during executive reviews.

Appendix note: legal reviewers may require minimization of raw PromQL in evidence packs; store hashes or UIDs instead of full expressions when policy demands.

Appendix note: duplicate scrapers of Grafana metrics inflate eval_total until dedupe rules land architecturally; compare scrape job cardinality before raising thresholds.

Appendix note: Grafana Enterprise audit logs complement this UC when operators need proof of provisioning edits during incident windows.

Appendix note: Grafana Cloud vendor maintenance can move duration envelopes fleet-wide; compare vendor status before internal blame during correlated spikes.

Appendix note: blue-green Alertmanager migrations may elevate alertmanager_dispatcher_aggregation_groups during cutover; treat as expected when change tickets align.

Appendix note: planned high-cardinality rule rollouts should carry pre-approval for temporary critical noise with documented rollback timeboxes.

Appendix note: network partition tests in chaos programs will spike timeouts and missed counters in non-production; suppress with environment tags.

Appendix note: scheduled large-table backfills in warehouses can slow datasource responses; correlate warehouse job schedules before rewriting rules.

Appendix note: ruler upgrades during approved windows should reference sustain_window_sec metadata from cmdb to downshift paging severity.

Appendix note: teach incident commanders that recommended_action tokens route to ngalert tuning, ruler sharding, kube capacity, or Alertmanager dispatcher review—not contact point rotation by default.

Appendix note: optional summary indexes keyed on grafana_org_id rule_uid reduce Job Inspector cost for hourly executive rollups without losing investigative fidelity.

Appendix note: service mesh retries can mask transient ruler errors; mesh dashboards belong beside this alert in the same portal page.

Appendix note: OpenTelemetry Collector forwarding of Grafana logs must preserve ngalert logger namespaces or the log arm goes quiet during real incidents.

Appendix note: Splunk ingest latency can delay five-minute buckets; monitor platform lag separately before muting perceived evaluation misses.

Appendix note: cross-region Grafana replicas need consistent inventory rows for rule_uid or joins return unassigned owners during legitimate crises.

Appendix note: document explicit owner for Mimir-rules CRD repositories so Grafana and ruler changes do not land without paired review.

Appendix note: recording rule promotion should include a checklist item referencing this UC so performance gains are measurable post-change.

Appendix note: when Grafana moves to new internal metric spellings, maintain a translation macro so historical dashboards remain comparable.

Appendix note: executive summaries can chart rolling thirty-day counts of eval_missed_count sustained above zero without exposing raw PromQL.

Appendix note: on-call runbook URLs in inventory should resolve without VPN surprises; test from bastion quarterly.

Appendix note: Grafana JSON provisioning for alert rules should include comments in Git reviews explaining interval choices tied to query cost.

Appendix note: Mimir query scheduler saturation sometimes presents as ruler latency first; keep querier CPU dashboards linked from this UC.

Appendix note: Loki ingester pressure can delay ruler queries against logs; pair with logging platform health cards.

Appendix note: Tempo block compaction spikes can slow metrics-generator reads; correlate compaction timelines when durations widen seasonally.

Appendix note: Prometheus tsdb WAL or head issues can slow rule reads; distinguish Prometheus health from Grafana health using sibling platform UCs.

Appendix note: ensure kubectl access policies allow on-call to describe pods referenced by kubernetes_runtime lanes without escalating to cluster-admin.

Appendix note: Grafana anonymous usage statistics are unrelated; do not confuse outbound telemetry with evaluation metrics when debugging network ACLs.

Appendix note: finalize training materials that show a single screenshot translating severity rows into three remediation branches: query cost, scheduler capacity, downstream Alertmanager backlog.
spl:
`comment("UC-13.6.3 Grafana ngalert rule evaluation duration concurrency timeout missed Mimir ruler kube alertmanager earliest=-4h@h bucket=5m stream_window=12h")`
| multisearch
    [ | mstats latest(_value) AS mv WHERE index=prometheus_metrics metric_name IN ("grafana_alerting_rule_evaluations_total","grafana_alerting_rule_evaluations_failed_total","grafana_alerting_rule_group_iterations_missed_total","grafana_alerting_scheduler_concurrent_evaluations") span=5m BY metric_name org_id rule_uid rule_group
      | eval grafana_org_id=trim(toString(coalesce(org_id,"0")))
      | eval rule_uid=trim(toString(coalesce(rule_uid,"unknown_rule")))
      | eval rule_group=trim(toString(coalesce(rule_group,"unknown_group")))
      | eval ruler_backend="grafana_ngalert"
      | eval eval_total=if(metric_name=="grafana_alerting_rule_evaluations_total",mv,null())
      | eval eval_failed=if(metric_name=="grafana_alerting_rule_evaluations_failed_total",mv,null())
      | eval eval_missed=if(metric_name=="grafana_alerting_rule_group_iterations_missed_total",mv,null())
      | eval scheduler_concurrent_evaluations=if(metric_name=="grafana_alerting_scheduler_concurrent_evaluations",mv,null())
      | eval group_eval_interval_ms=null()
      | eval rule_lat=null()
      | eval ruler_query_p95_ms=null()
      | eval alertmanager_send_lag_ms=null()
      | eval restarts_count=null()
      | fields _time grafana_org_id rule_group rule_uid ruler_backend eval_total eval_failed eval_missed scheduler_concurrent_evaluations group_eval_interval_ms rule_lat ruler_query_p95_ms alertmanager_send_lag_ms restarts_count ]
    [ search (index=grafana OR index=observability OR index=platform OR index=main) sourcetype=grafana:server earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval lr=lower(_raw)
      | where (like(lr,"%lvl=eror%") OR like(lr,"%level=error%") OR like(lr,"%lvl=error%")) AND (like(lr,"%ngalert%") OR match(lr,"rule evaluation failed|evaluation timed out|evaluat.*fail|context deadline exceeded"))
      | rex field=lr max_match=1 "(?i)(?:duration|took|elapsed)[^0-9]*(?<rule_lat>[0-9]{1,9})(?:\\.\\d+)?\\s*ms"
      | eval rule_lat=if(isnotnull(rule_lat),tonumber(rule_lat,10),null())
      | eval grafana_org_id=trim(toString(coalesce(orgId,org_id,org,"0")))
      | eval rule_uid=trim(toString(coalesce(rule_uid,ruleUID,ruleUid,"unknown_rule")))
      | eval rule_group=trim(toString(coalesce(rule_group,group,"unknown_group")))
      | eval ruler_backend="grafana_ngalert"
      | bucket _time span=5m
      | stats perc95(rule_lat) AS rule_lat count AS log_err BY _time grafana_org_id rule_group rule_uid ruler_backend
      | eval eval_total=null() | eval eval_failed=null() | eval eval_missed=null() | eval scheduler_concurrent_evaluations=null()
      | eval group_eval_interval_ms=null() | eval ruler_query_p95_ms=null() | eval alertmanager_send_lag_ms=null() | eval restarts_count=null()
      | fields _time grafana_org_id rule_group rule_uid ruler_backend eval_total eval_failed eval_missed scheduler_concurrent_evaluations group_eval_interval_ms rule_lat ruler_query_p95_ms alertmanager_send_lag_ms restarts_count ]
    [ search (index=prometheus_metrics OR index=metrics OR index=observability_metrics) sourcetype=prometheus:metrics earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval mn=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(__name__,metric_name,""))))
      | where match(mn,"cortex_ruler_.*query|cortex_ruler_rules|cortex_alertmanager_alerts")
      | eval grafana_org_id=trim(toString(coalesce(user,org_id,"0")))
      | eval rule_uid=trim(toString(coalesce(rule_uid,ruler_pod,"mimir_ruler_shard")))
      | eval rule_group=trim(toString(coalesce(rule_group,"cortex_ruler_group")))
      | eval ruler_backend="mimir_cortex"
      | eval v=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(Value,value,metric_value,"0")),10)
      | eval ruler_query_p95_ms=if(match(mn,"seconds|duration|latency"),v*1000,v)
      | bucket _time span=5m
      | stats perc95(ruler_query_p95_ms) AS ruler_query_p95_ms BY _time grafana_org_id rule_group rule_uid ruler_backend
      | eval eval_total=null() | eval eval_failed=null() | eval eval_missed=null() | eval scheduler_concurrent_evaluations=null()
      | eval group_eval_interval_ms=null() | eval rule_lat=null() | eval alertmanager_send_lag_ms=null() | eval restarts_count=null()
      | fields _time grafana_org_id rule_group rule_uid ruler_backend eval_total eval_failed eval_missed scheduler_concurrent_evaluations group_eval_interval_ms rule_lat ruler_query_p95_ms alertmanager_send_lag_ms restarts_count ]
    [ | mstats latest(_value) AS mv WHERE index=prometheus_metrics metric_name IN ("container_memory_working_set_bytes","kube_pod_container_status_restarts_total") span=5m BY pod namespace metric_name
      | where match(lower(pod),"grafana|mimir-ruler|cortex-ruler|ruler|grafana-k8s")
      | eval grafana_org_id="0"
      | eval rule_uid=trim(toString(pod))
      | eval rule_group=trim(toString(namespace))
      | eval ruler_backend="kubernetes_runtime"
      | eval restarts_count=if(metric_name=="kube_pod_container_status_restarts_total",mv,null())
      | eval eval_total=null() | eval eval_failed=null() | eval eval_missed=null() | eval scheduler_concurrent_evaluations=null()
      | eval group_eval_interval_ms=null() | eval rule_lat=null() | eval ruler_query_p95_ms=null() | eval alertmanager_send_lag_ms=null()
      | fields _time grafana_org_id rule_group rule_uid ruler_backend eval_total eval_failed eval_missed scheduler_concurrent_evaluations group_eval_interval_ms rule_lat ruler_query_p95_ms alertmanager_send_lag_ms restarts_count ]
    [ search (index=prometheus_metrics OR index=metrics OR index=observability_metrics) sourcetype=prometheus:metrics earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval mn=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(__name__,metric_name,""))))
      | where match(mn,"alertmanager_dispatcher_aggregation_groups|alertmanager_notification_latency_seconds|alertmanager_alerts")
      | eval grafana_org_id="0"
      | eval rule_uid="alertmanager_global"
      | eval rule_group="alertmanager"
      | eval ruler_backend="prometheus_alertmanager"
      | eval v=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(Value,value,metric_value,"0")),10)
      | eval alertmanager_send_lag_ms=if(match(mn,"latency|seconds"),v*1000,v)
      | bucket _time span=5m
      | stats max(alertmanager_send_lag_ms) AS alertmanager_send_lag_ms BY _time grafana_org_id rule_group rule_uid ruler_backend
      | eval eval_total=null() | eval eval_failed=null() | eval eval_missed=null() | eval scheduler_concurrent_evaluations=null()
      | eval group_eval_interval_ms=null() | eval rule_lat=null() | eval ruler_query_p95_ms=null() | eval restarts_count=null()
      | fields _time grafana_org_id rule_group rule_uid ruler_backend eval_total eval_failed eval_missed scheduler_concurrent_evaluations group_eval_interval_ms rule_lat ruler_query_p95_ms alertmanager_send_lag_ms restarts_count ]
| eval grafana_org_id=coalesce(grafana_org_id,"0")
| eval rule_uid=coalesce(rule_uid,"unknown_rule")
| eval rule_group=coalesce(rule_group,"unknown_group")
| eval ruler_backend=coalesce(ruler_backend,"grafana_ngalert")
| stats sum(eval_total) AS eval_total sum(eval_failed) AS eval_failed sum(eval_missed) AS eval_missed max(scheduler_concurrent_evaluations) AS scheduler_concurrent_evaluations max(group_eval_interval_ms) AS group_eval_interval_ms perc95(rule_lat) AS rule_eval_p95_ms max(ruler_query_p95_ms) AS ruler_query_p95_ms max(alertmanager_send_lag_ms) AS alertmanager_send_lag_ms max(restarts_count) AS restarts_count BY _time grafana_org_id rule_group rule_uid ruler_backend
| sort 0 grafana_org_id rule_group rule_uid ruler_backend - _time
| streamstats window=12 current=t global=f sum(eval_missed) AS win_missed sum(eval_failed) AS win_fail sum(eval_total) AS win_tot BY grafana_org_id rule_group rule_uid
| eval eval_missed_count=win_missed
| eval eval_failed_rate=round(100*win_fail/nullif(win_tot,0),3)
| eval group_eval_interval_ms=if(isnull(group_eval_interval_ms) OR group_eval_interval_ms<1,60000,group_eval_interval_ms)
| eventstats avg(eval_total) AS org_eval_baseline BY grafana_org_id
| eval rule_eval_p95_ms=coalesce(rule_eval_p95_ms,0)
| eval ruler_query_p95_ms=coalesce(ruler_query_p95_ms,0)
| eval alertmanager_send_lag_ms=coalesce(alertmanager_send_lag_ms,0)
| eval restarts_count=coalesce(restarts_count,0)
| eval severity=case((eval_missed_count>=2) OR (eval_failed_rate>5) OR (rule_eval_p95_ms>(group_eval_interval_ms*0.8)),"critical", eval_failed_rate>2 OR rule_eval_p95_ms>(group_eval_interval_ms*0.5) OR ruler_query_p95_ms>800 OR alertmanager_send_lag_ms>1500 OR restarts_count>=3,"high", eval_failed_rate>0.5 OR rule_eval_p95_ms>(group_eval_interval_ms*0.35) OR scheduler_concurrent_evaluations>80,"medium", true(),"low")
| eval recommended_action=case(severity=="critical" AND eval_missed_count>=2,"inspect_rule_group_interval_eval_duration_raise_concurrency_shard_rules", severity=="critical" AND eval_failed_rate>5,"isolate_toxic_promql_validate_recording_rules_and_datasource_slo", match(ruler_backend,"mimir"),"rebalance_mimir_ruler_query_path_review_ruler_crds_and_shard_imbalance", restarts_count>=3,"remediate_grafana_or_ruler_pod_oom_crashloop_kube_resources", alertmanager_send_lag_ms>1500,"tune_alertmanager_dispatcher_fanout_correlate_downstream_queue_not_notifier_uc", true(),"review_unified_alerting_remote_eval_toggles_and_ha_leader_election_logs")
| join type=left max=0 rule_uid [| inputlookup grafana_rule_inventory.csv | eval rule_uid=trim(toString(coalesce(rule_uid,uid,""))) | fields rule_uid owner_team business_service on_call_runbook_url group_eval_interval_ms_hint]
| join type=left rule_uid [| search index=cmdb sourcetype=grafana_rule_metadata earliest=-24h latest=now | dedup rule_uid | fields rule_uid sustain_window_sec service_tier ruler_backend_meta]
| eval group_eval_interval_ms=coalesce(group_eval_interval_ms_hint,group_eval_interval_ms)
| eval ruler_backend=coalesce(ruler_backend_meta,ruler_backend)
| eval owner_team=coalesce(owner_team,"unassigned")
| eval business_service=coalesce(business_service,"unknown_service")
| table _time grafana_org_id ruler_backend rule_group rule_uid owner_team business_service rule_eval_p95_ms group_eval_interval_ms eval_failed_rate eval_missed_count ruler_query_p95_ms alertmanager_send_lag_ms severity recommended_action
cimSpl:
| tstats summariesonly=true latest(Alerts.severity) AS sev count FROM datamodel=Alerts WHERE nodename=Alerts earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Alerts.signature
| rename Alerts.signature AS rule_sig
| join type=left max=0 rule_sig [| tstats summariesonly=true latest(Performance.cpu_load_percent) AS cpu_load latest(Performance.mem_used_percent) AS mem_pct FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Performance.host | rename Performance.host AS rule_sig]
| table rule_sig sev cpu_load mem_pct
dataModelAcceleration: Accelerate Alerts summaries when Grafana firing events map to CIM Alerts signatures; accelerate Performance summaries for hosts mapped as ruler or Grafana nodes when correlating evaluation lag with CPU and memory pressure; retain thirty days for quarterly observability attestations.
visualization: Severity-colored table by grafana_org_id, rule_group, and rule_uid with drilldowns to mstats rows, grafana:server errors, cortex ruler metrics, kube pod series, and Alertmanager gauges; heatmap of eval_failed_rate; timeline of rule_eval_p95_ms against group_eval_interval_ms.

references.title: Kubernetes — Pod metrics and resource monitoring

knownFalsePositives: Planned Mimir or Cortex ruler upgrades can spike ruler_query_p95_ms and missed counters until shards stabilize; require change_ticket correlation before paging application owners. Deliberate high-cardinality rule rollouts in non-production will move duration histograms without customer impact; tag lab org identifiers and suppress production routing. Chaos network partition tests elevate timeout logs and missed iterations in sandbox clusters; annotate drill metadata on alerts. Scheduled large-table warehouse backfills can slow datasource responses and widen evaluation duration without Grafana defects; correlate warehouse job schedules before rewriting rules. Blue-green migrations to a new Alertmanager pair may elevate alertmanager_dispatcher_aggregation_groups during cutover windows; compare against approved maintenance calendars. Canary rules that intentionally exercise expensive PromQL raise eval_failed_rate briefly; gate with inventory flags. Certificate rotations on scrape targets can drop mstats arms while logs remain quiet; fix scrape health before muting. Duplicate Prometheus scrapers of Grafana metrics inflate eval_total denominators; dedupe architecturally. Grafana Cloud regional incidents can widen duration percentiles across many tenants simultaneously; validate vendor status before internal blame. Penetration testing traffic against Grafana APIs can increase reload churn; maintain test IP allowlists where possible. Very low evaluation counts per bucket produce unstable rates; require minimum eval_total floors in alert wrappers beyond this exploratory search.
hardware: Applies to Grafana containers on Kubernetes, Linux virtual machines running Grafana OSS or Enterprise, managed Grafana Cloud stacks, Mimir or Cortex ruler pods, Prometheus Alertmanager deployments, and hybrid cloud footprints scraping metrics into Splunk.
exclusions: Does not replace UC-13.6.2 data source proxy query error analytics, UC-13.6.13 contact point delivery failure analytics, per-panel dashboard load latency cards in isolation, or deep PromQL cost optimization workshops without evaluation engine signals.

controlTest.positiveScenario: In a lab Grafana stack with Unified Alerting, configure a canary rule with an intentionally slow or timing-out query, confirm grafana:server ngalert errors or rising grafana_alerting_rule_evaluations_failed_total, and verify observability_uc_13_6_3_grafana_ngalert_rule_eval_engine returns critical or high within two five-minute windows with non-null recommended_action and populated rule_uid after inventory join.
controlTest.negativeScenario: After restoring fast queries, healthy scrape paths, quiet missed counters, and steady scheduler gauges, confirm severities remain low for steady state and the alert wrapper emits zero rows when filtering to critical and high only for thirty consecutive runs.

evidence: Saved search observability_uc_13_6_3_grafana_ngalert_rule_eval_engine on a five-minute schedule; observability dashboard drilldowns across metrics, logs, ruler, kube, and Alertmanager lanes; alert routing to platform observability on-call; weekly CSV exports archived to a restricted evidence index.
